DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT KENNETH REICH'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO DECEMBER 12, 2012 AT 9:00 AM TO GIVE DEFENDANTS THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. DEFENDANTS MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF ITS MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 28, 2012 AT 4 PM, OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. IT APPEARS NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED. THE ONLY COURTESY COPIES THAT THE COURT RECEIVED WERE DEFENDANTS' PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

DISCOVERY 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT KENNETH REICH'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS WAIVED ALL OBJECTIONS TO DEFENDANT'S FORM INTERROGATORIES - GENERAL, SET ONE AND DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE. WITHIN 14 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER, PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE VERIFIED CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S FORM INTERROGATORIES AND DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION, WITHOUT OBJECTION. SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $620.00 ARE AWARDED. PLAINTIFF SHALL PAY DEFENDANT $620.00 WITHIN 14 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED. ERNST HALPERIN, JUDGE PRO-TEM, JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, CLERK, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Respoonses To Supplemental Interrogatory And Supplemental Demand For Prodcution Of Documents And Award Of Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $620.00
Off Calendar Jun-28-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances on FRI-JUN-28-2013:
Sonia C. Merida, Esq. of Law offices of Santana & Hart [415.777.1308] appearing by telephone for KENNETH REICH, defendant / moving party; No other appearances.

HEARING - R U L I N G re: Defendant KENNETH REICH'S Motion to Compel Responses to Supplemental Interrogatory and Supplemental Demand for Prodcution of Documents and Award of Monetary Sanctions in the Amount of $620.00. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: Defendant KENNETH REICH'S motion is Granted. No opposition filed. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $330. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Bradley P. Kaplan; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)

MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES
Off Calendar Dec-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Sonia C. Merida, 415.777.1308, appearing for the Defendant;
No appearance by any other counsel or party.

Ruling: the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT KENNETH REICH'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES is GRANTED. Plaintiff has waived all objections to Defendant's Form Interrogatories - General, Set One and Demand for Production of Documents, Set One. Within 14 days of entry of this order, plaintiff shall serve verified code-compliant responses to Defendant's Form Interrogatories and Demand for Production, without objection. Sanctions in the amount of $620.00 are awarded. Plaintiff shall pay Defendant $620.00 within 14 days of entry of this order. Order signed. Ernst Halperin, Judge Pro-Tem, Jacqueline Alameda, Clerk, Not Reported. =302/JPT

MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES
Off Calendar Nov-28-2012 Continued To Dec-12-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT KENNETH REICH'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES
No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Continued on the court's own motion to December 12, 2012 at 9:00 am to give Defendants the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Defendants must deliver courtesy copies of its moving papers to Department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date no later than November 28, 2012 at 4 pm, or the motion will be denied. It appears no opposition has been filed. The only courtesy copies that the Court received were Defendants' proposed order. (302/JPT) Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not reported
